text:
HEMATOPATHOLOGY REPORT
Division of Hematopathology and Bone Marrow Pathology

---

PATIENT: Vasquez-Okonkwo, Renata
SEX: Female
AGE: 49 years
CLINICAL SERVICE: Allogeneic Stem Cell Transplant / Leukemia
ATTENDING PHYSICIAN: [Transplant/Leukemia Attending]
SPECIMEN SUBMITTED BY: Hematology-Oncology / Transplant Team

CLINICAL HISTORY: 49-year-old female with history of acute myeloid leukemia (AML), NOS, FAB M1, with high allelic ratio FLT3-ITD (allelic ratio 0.91), DNMT3A R882H, TET2 Q1299*, WT1 R462W co-mutations, and trisomy 8 on cytogenetics (ELN 2022 Adverse risk). She was treated with 7+3 induction (cytarabine/daunorubicin) plus midostaurin, achieving MRD-negative complete remission after first induction. She subsequently underwent myeloablative conditioning allogeneic peripheral blood stem cell transplantation from a 10/10 HLA-matched unrelated donor (fludarabine/busulfan conditioning, tacrolimus/MTX GVHD prophylaxis). She has had prior day +30 and day +180 bone marrow biopsies, both demonstrating sustained MRD-negative CR with full donor chimerism. This specimen is submitted for the 12-month (day +365) post-transplant annual surveillance bone marrow assessment.

---

SPECIMEN INFORMATION

Specimen ID: [LAB ACCESSION NUMBER]
Date of Procedure: [DATE]
Type of Procedure: Bilateral posterior superior iliac crest bone marrow biopsy and aspirate (right-sided core biopsy and clot section; bilateral aspirates)
Fixative/Processing: Right posterior iliac crest core biopsy fixed in B-Plus fixative, decalcified, paraffin-embedded; aspirate smears air-dried and fixed in methanol; clot section in formalin, paraffin-embedded.
Special Studies Submitted: Flow cytometry MRD immunophenotyping (10-color panel), FLT3-ITD qualitative PCR (bone marrow and peripheral blood), WT1 quantitative RT-PCR, DNMT3A R882H allele-specific PCR, STR chimerism analysis (peripheral blood).

---

GROSS PATHOLOGY

Right posterior iliac crest core biopsy: A single core of pale tan-pink trabecular bone and marrow tissue measuring 1.8 cm in length x 0.2 cm in diameter is received in B-Plus fixative. The specimen is processed in its entirety. Representative sections obtained.

Bone marrow aspirate: Adequate aspirate specimens received from the right and left posterior superior iliac crest. Aspirate smears demonstrate good spicule representation with adequate particle cellularity for morphologic assessment and flow cytometry. Material was triaged for morphology, cytochemistry if indicated, and ancillary studies.

Aspiration clot: A pale gray-pink soft tissue clot measuring 0.6 x 0.4 x 0.3 cm. Processed in entirety.

---

DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS

I. BONE MARROW BIOPSY (Core, right PSIS)

Cellularity: The biopsy is normocellular, with an estimated overall cellularity of 45-50%, appropriate for patient age. Bone trabeculae are present and appear intact without evidence of abnormal remodeling or fibrosis by H&E assessment. A reticulin stain (MF grading) demonstrates minimal fibrosis, MF-0 (no increase in reticulin fibers; normal meshwork), markedly improved from the markedly fibrotic marrow that was anticipated at diagnosis but consistent with progressive normalization throughout the post-transplant course.

Hematopoiesis: Trilineage hematopoiesis is present and morphologically normal. Erythropoiesis is adequate, with orderly maturation from pronormoblasts through orthochromatic normoblasts without dysplastic features (no megaloblastic change, no nuclear irregularity, no cytoplasmic bridging). Granulopoiesis is present in normal proportions, with left-shifted but orderly maturation through band forms and segmented neutrophils; no dysgranulopoiesis (no hypogranular or hyposegmented forms, no pseudo-Pelger-Huet anomaly, no abnormal nuclear contours). Megakaryocytes are present in normal numbers, with adequate representation of mature, multilobed megakaryocytes; no clustering, no micromegakaryocytes, no abnormal nuclear forms. Lymphoid nodules: One small, well-circumscribed perivascular lymphoid aggregate is identified, composed of predominantly small mature lymphocytes; this finding is nonspecific and may represent residual or reactive lymphoid tissue in the post-transplant setting.

Blast assessment: No morphologic increase in blasts. CD34 immunohistochemistry (performed on the core biopsy): rare CD34-positive cells, estimated at less than 1% of nucleated marrow cells, distributed in the normal paratrabecular pattern without clustering or sheet formation. This is consistent with normal hematopoietic progenitor representation. CD117 (c-Kit) immunohistochemistry: scattered positivity in myeloid precursors and rare mast cells; no aberrant clustering or increase.

Additional immunohistochemistry: CD3 highlights scattered T-lymphocytes in normal distribution; CD20 highlights rare B-lymphocytes; MPO highlights maturing myeloid elements throughout with normal distribution. No evidence of lymphomatous or plasma cell infiltrate.

Conclusion (core biopsy): Normocellular bone marrow with complete trilineage hematopoiesis. No morphologic evidence of residual or recurrent acute myeloid leukemia. CD34 within normal limits. MF-0 reticulin.

---

II. BONE MARROW ASPIRATE

Smear quality: Adequate; multiple spicule-bearing particles present. 500-cell differential count performed.

500-Cell Aspirate Differential (% of nucleated cells):
  Myeloblasts: 1.4%
  Promyelocytes: 3.2%
  Myelocytes: 9.6%
  Metamyelocytes: 8.8%
  Band forms: 14.2%
  Segmented neutrophils: 17.4%
  Eosinophils: 2.8%
  Basophils: 0.4%
  Monocytes: 1.6%
  Lymphocytes: 14.8%
  Plasma cells: 0.6%
  Proerythroblasts: 2.4%
  Basophilic normoblasts: 5.6%
  Polychromatophilic normoblasts: 9.8%
  Orthochromatic normoblasts: 7.4%
  M:E ratio: approximately 2.1:1 (within normal range)

Myeloblast morphology: The 1.4% myeloblasts identified are morphologically normal, with regular nuclear contours, inconspicuous nucleoli, and scant cytoplasm. No Auer rods are identified. No cytoplasmic granule abnormalities. These are entirely consistent with normal hematopoietic progenitors. Importantly, no abnormal blasts with the morphologic features of the original leukemic clone (large cells with irregular nuclei, prominent nucleoli, pale abundant cytoplasm, and Auer rods) are identified.

Dysplasia assessment: No significant dyserythropoiesis (dysplastic forms < 5% of erythroid precursors -- within acceptable limits given post-transplant state and prior chemotherapy). No dysmegakaryopoiesis. No dysgranulopoiesis meeting WHO criteria. Overall: no morphologic dysplasia sufficient to suggest myelodysplastic syndrome.

Conclusion (aspirate): Maturing trilineage hematopoiesis. Myeloblasts 1.4% (within normal limits). No morphologic evidence of AML.

---

III. ANCILLARY STUDIES

A. Flow Cytometry MRD Immunophenotyping
Method: 10-color multiparameter flow cytometry, minimum 500,000 nucleated events acquired.
Panel antibodies: CD45, CD34, CD117, CD13, CD33, CD123, HLA-DR, CD16, CD11b, CD56, MPO, CD19, CD3, TdT, CD64, CD14, CD38, CD138 (two tubes, leukemia-directed MRD and broad immunophenotypic characterization).

Results: No aberrant blast population identified. The CD34+/CD117+ progenitor compartment accounts for less than 0.1% of viable CD45+ events (within normal limits). No abnormal immunophenotypic features characteristic of the original AML clone (CD34+/CD117+/CD13+/CD33+/CD123+/HLA-DR+/MPO+ with aberrant co-expression profile) are present at any level of detection. Leukemia-associated immunophenotype (LAIP)-based MRD: NEGATIVE. Sensitivity: 0.01% (1 in 10,000 nucleated events).

B. Molecular Studies

FLT3-ITD Qualitative PCR (bone marrow aspirate):
Method: Fragment analysis by capillary electrophoresis, sensitivity approximately 1 in 10,000 (10^-4).
Result: FLT3-ITD NOT DETECTED. Wild-type FLT3 fragment present (expected product at 329 bp). No mutant FLT3-ITD product detected above the limit of detection. This represents continued undetectable FLT3-ITD from the original clone with the 33-base pair tandem duplication.

WT1 Quantitative RT-PCR (bone marrow aspirate, EAC reference standard):
Result: WT1 transcript level = 9 copies / 10,000 ABL1 copies.
Interpretation: Within normal range (institutional reference: < 50 copies / 10,000 ABL1 copies for bone marrow). NEGATIVE for WT1 overexpression consistent with residual/relapsing AML.

DNMT3A R882H Allele-Specific PCR (peripheral blood):
Method: Allele-specific PCR with sensitivity approximately 1 in 1,000 (10^-3).
Result: DNMT3A R882H NOT DETECTED. This mutation (originally identified at VAF 49.8%, consistent with a founder CHIP-origin clone) is now below the limit of detection, indicating eradication of the original founding leukemic clone by the allograft.

C. STR Chimerism Analysis (Peripheral Blood, Day +365)
Method: Short tandem repeat (STR) polymorphism analysis, 15-locus panel (AmpFLSTR Identifiler Plus), performed on peripheral blood.

Results:
  Overall chimerism (unfractionated peripheral blood): 99.9% donor origin
  CD3+ T-cell fraction chimerism: 99.2% donor origin
  CD33+ myeloid fraction chimerism: 100.0% donor origin

Interpretation: Complete donor chimerism across all hematopoietic lineages assessed. The CD33+ myeloid compartment shows 100% donor engraftment, consistent with full replacement of host hematopoiesis. No evidence of mixed chimerism or autologous (host) hematopoietic recovery that would raise concern for graft failure or early relapse. The CD3+ T-cell chimerism of 99.2% donor represents near-complete donor T-cell reconstitution, improved from 89% at day +21, 95.2% at day +30, and 98.1% at day +180.

---

OVERALL INTERPRETATION AND CONCLUSION

This 12-month (day +365) post-allogeneic stem cell transplant bone marrow assessment demonstrates:

1. Normocellular bone marrow (45-50% cellularity) with complete, morphologically normal trilineage hematopoiesis.
2. No morphologic evidence of residual or recurrent acute myeloid leukemia (AML). Myeloblasts 1.4% by aspirate differential (normal); CD34 less than 1% by IHC.
3. Flow cytometry MRD: NEGATIVE (sensitivity 0.01%); no leukemia-associated immunophenotype detected.
4. FLT3-ITD PCR: NOT DETECTED (sensitivity 10^-4).
5. WT1 quantitative PCR: 9 copies/10,000 ABL1 copies -- within normal limits.
6. DNMT3A R882H allele-specific PCR: NOT DETECTED -- eradication of founding clone confirmed.
7. Complete donor chimerism: overall 99.9%, myeloid 100%, T-cell 99.2% donor.
8. MF-0 reticulin (no marrow fibrosis).

DIAGNOSIS: COMPLETE MORPHOLOGIC AND MOLECULAR REMISSION WITH SUSTAINED MRD NEGATIVITY AND COMPLETE DONOR CHIMERISM AT 12 MONTHS POST-ALLOGENEIC STEM CELL TRANSPLANTATION. No morphologic or molecular evidence of AML relapse.

---

SIGNED BY: [Attending Hematopathologist]
PATHOLOGY ATTENDING: [Name], MD
Division of Hematopathology

Electronic signature applied upon final sign-out.

Addendum: Peripheral blood CBC at time of specimen collection (provided by clinical team for correlation): WBC 6,100/uL, ANC 3,820/uL, hemoglobin 13.1 g/dL, MCV 88 fL, platelets 231,000/uL. CBC is within normal limits and entirely consistent with the excellent morphologic and molecular remission status documented herein.
